name: Chili Shrimp with Basil
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Unsalted roasted peanuts, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
---------------------------SAUCE---------------------------
2 tbsp Fish sauce
2 tbsp Lime juice
2 tbsp Water
2 tsp Sugar
1 tsp Chinese chili sauce
2 Garlic cloves, finely minced
2 tbsp Chopped fresh basil
1. Shell the shrimp and devein by cutting along the top of the curve, starting at the tail and making a progressively deeper cut until the knife nearly cuts through at the thick end. Rinse out the vein.
2. Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a rapid boil and add the shrimp. Cook until shrimp are done (1-2 minutes), checking by cutting a shrimp in half; it should be white inside.
3. Transfer shrimp immediately to a bowl of ice water to cool. When chilled, drain and refrigerate until ready to use.
4. Mince red pepper and chives, placing them in separate containers.
5. Finely chop peanuts in a food processor and set aside.
6. Combine sauce ingredients and mix well.
7. Toss shrimp with the sauce. Place in a decorative bowl over crushed ice.
8. Sprinkle shrimp with red pepper, chives, and peanuts.
9. Serve at once.
Serves: 4 to 8 as an appetizer or 4 as a first course.
